_comment: A small, self-contained scenario for the steward demo (#324).
It mirrors the four shipped single-invariant stewards in fak/internal/steward/steward.go
and the meta-steward prune (units 87-92 in fak/internal/steward/steward_test.go).
The load-bearing field is `witness`. A steward returns (violated, witness). It only
RAISES when both (a) its one-sentence invariant pattern matches AND (b) the `witness`
field is non-empty -- and a witness is authored by an INDEPENDENT scan, never by the
thing under test. A pattern match with an empty witness is a self-accusation the model
cannot make, so the steward ABSTAINS. That is the whole trust model: you do not trust
the alert, you trust the witness chain behind it.
Each `probe` below is a frozen capture of what the real steward's check function would
observe at runtime (the context snapshot, the live lease set, the KPI pair, the vDSO
probe). `witness_author` records WHO produced the witness, so the demo can show the
difference between an independently-corroborated raise and an un-witnessed (suppressed)

stewards.name: no-secret-in-context
stewards.invariant: no secret-shaped bytes are ever admitted to the model's context window

cases.label: secret admitted, independently scanned -> RAISE

context_snapshot: user: please use token sk-abcdef0123456789abcdef0123 for the call

cases.witness: secret-shaped bytes admitted to context: sk-abcde…
cases.witness_author: secretscan (independent regex scanner over the live context snapshot)

cases.label: same pattern, NO independent scan authored a witness -> SUPPRESSED

context_snapshot: user: please use token sk-abcdef0123456789abcdef0123 for the call

cases.witness_author: model-self-report (the agent claiming about its own context; not trusted)

cases.label: clean context, nothing to scan -> ABSTAIN
cases.witness_author: secretscan (independent regex scanner over the live context snapshot)

stewards.name: lease-disjointness
stewards.invariant: no two live file-tree leases share a path prefix

cases.label: two leases collide on a tree prefix, arbiter confirms -> RAISE
cases.witness: a and b collide on internal/abi/internal/abi/types
cases.witness_author: arbiter (independent lease registry replay, not either worker)

cases.label: fully disjoint leases -> ABSTAIN
cases.witness_author: arbiter (independent lease registry replay, not either worker)

stewards.name: kpi-regression
stewards.invariant: the current p50 never regresses past the committed baseline by more than tol

cases.label: current p50 doubled vs committed baseline, scoreboard confirms -> RAISE
cases.witness_author: scoreboard (independent committed-baseline reader, not the run reporting its own number)

cases.label: current == baseline -> ABSTAIN
cases.witness_author: scoreboard (independent committed-baseline reader, not the run reporting its own number)

stewards.name: vdso-soundness
stewards.invariant: a cached-path result is bit-identical to a fresh pure recompute

cases.label: cache hit disagrees with a fresh recompute, replayer confirms -> RAISE
cases.witness: vdso cache hit != fresh pure call (recompute disagreed)
cases.witness_author: replayer (independent fresh recompute of the same pure call, side-by-side)

cases.label: cache hit equals the fresh recompute -> ABSTAIN
cases.witness_author: replayer (independent fresh recompute of the same pure call, side-by-side)

_comment: The meta-steward (Population.Prune, unit 92) is dead-code detection for the
invariant layer itself: after a soak it removes any steward that NEVER fired, so
the population cannot ossify into checks that can no longer trip. This fixture has
one steward wired to a probe it can never match -- the soak proves it dead and the

population.name: clock-monotonic
population.why_dead: guards a clock-skew invariant the platform made impossible; the probe can no longer trip, so the steward is dead weight
